activities_projects.title: Case Study: Lifestyle and Mental Health
activities_projects.objective: Analyze how lifestyle and environment affect mental health
steps: Read the case study of the student who moved to a new city
Identify the factors that caused his health problems
Suggest three lifestyle changes that could improve his physical and mental health
Explain how social relationships influence overall well-being
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students critically analyze how social and lifestyle factors affect health
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of analysis and reasoning (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Health Data Interpretation
steps: Use the dengue case data given in the chapter
Create a bar graph showing the number of cases each month
Identify the peak months of dengue
Explain environmental factors responsible for the increase
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn to interpret data and relate it to environmental conditions
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of graph and logical conclusions (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Communicable Disease Transmission Model
steps: Choose a communicable disease such as malaria or cholera
Create a flow diagram showing how the disease spreads
Identify each stage of transmission and prevention methods
Present the model to the class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand disease transmission cycles and prevention strategies
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of model and explanation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Antibiotic Resistance Investigation
activities_projects.objective: Understand the causes and consequences of antibiotic resistance
steps: Research how misuse of antibiotics leads to resistance
Explain how resistant bacteria spread in communities
Suggest at least four ways to prevent antibiotic resistance
Prepare a short report
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop awareness about responsible antibiotic use
activities_projects.evaluation: Research quality and scientific explanation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Community Sanitation Impact Study
activities_projects.objective: Evaluate the role of sanitation in disease prevention
steps: Study the sanitation campaign example in the chapter
Explain how improved sanitation reduces disease transmission
Identify sanitation problems in your neighbourhood
Suggest practical solutions
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students analyze public health measures and their impact
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of analysis and recommendations (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Debate: Mandatory Vaccination
activities_projects.objective: Evaluate arguments related to vaccination policies
steps: Divide the class into two groups
One group argues for mandatory vaccination and the other against
Use scientific evidence to support arguments
Conduct a structured debate and summarize conclusions
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop critical thinking and scientific argumentation skills
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of arguments and participation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Lifestyle Disease Risk Assessment
activities_projects.objective: Analyze risk factors for non-communicable diseases
steps: Conduct a survey of five adults about lifestyle habits
Record diet, physical activity, and stress levels
Identify risk factors linked to diseases like diabetes or hypertension
Summarize findings
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students connect lifestyle choices with disease risk
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of survey and interpretation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Immunity and Vaccination Research Project
activities_projects.objective: Understand the functioning of the immune system
steps: Research how vaccines stimulate the immune system
Explain the difference between natural immunity and acquired immunity
Provide examples of vaccines used in childhood
Write a short explanatory report
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand advanced concepts of immunity

activities_projects.title: Health Campaign Design
activities_projects.objective: Create a comprehensive public health awareness campaign
steps: Identify a major health issue affecting students
Design posters, slogans, and awareness messages
Explain preventive measures for communicable and non-communicable diseases
Present the campaign plan to the class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop communication and public health planning skills

activities_projects.title: Health Diary Data Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Evaluate personal health habits using recorded data
steps: Maintain a health diary for two weeks
Record diet, exercise, sleep, screen time, and emotional state
Analyze patterns that influence health
Suggest improvements based on scientific reasoning
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop self-assessment and analytical skills
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of analysis and realistic recommendations (10 marks)
